page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s2.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the ecs.s2 server family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical CPU cores and 2 vCPUs, operating with a single thread per core configuration. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

This server configuration does not include local storage or bundled GPU resources, requiring external network storage volumes for operating system and application deployment. The CPU lacks support for advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512), Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), and hardware-accelerated cryptography standards such as AES-NI.

From a resource density perspective, the instance provides a memory-optimized profile with dedicated CPU allocation. This makes it suitable for lightweight memory-bound workloads, small database nodes, or caching layers that require dedicated compute cycles without the overhead of hyperthreading, though it lacks the vector processing capabilities needed for intensive mathematical or cryptographic operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s2.2xlarge is an x86_64 virtual machine instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, configured with a single thread per core on the KVM hypervisor. This instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it a memory-optimized option for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading. It lacks local storage, bundled GPUs, and advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512, and AES cryptography. The instance is designed for lightweight memory-bound applications, small database hosting, and caching layers where dedicated CPU allocation is preferred over shared-core alternatives.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s2.2xlarge is a KVM-based x86_64 instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight memory-optimized workloads.

tagline: A dedicated two-core x86_64 virtual server featuring sixteen gigabytes of system memory and a single-thread-per-core architecture.

KVM hypervisor on x86_64 CPU architecture
